method_atom: a bounded finite-structure search that decides whether a set of formal constraints is satisfiable over a fixed finite domain by grounding the constraints into a purely propositional (Boolean) constraint set and solving it with a systematic backtracking Davis-Putnam-Logemann-Loveland satisfiability procedure, then lifting any satisfying assignment back to a full model; it complements deductive proof search by supplying explicit countermodels when no proof exists
object_atom: first-order logical statements and their finite models, produced as a companion to the Otter automated theorem prover which searches for proofs while this tool searches for countermodels
brief_reason: the reduce-to-propositional-SAT-over-a-bounded-domain-then-lift-solutions search machinery is the transferable method, while first-order logic formulas, finite model finding, and the Otter theorem-prover pairing are the logic-specific object.
